---
title: "Biostatistics with Python"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-banner}

# Biostatistics with Python

## Learning Statistics Through Cardiovascular Data

Using the UCI Heart Disease dataset, this module introduces practical statistical reasoning for biomedical data analysis.

We will move from raw clinical measurements to visual summaries, statistical comparisons, and interpretable models.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Core question"}
How can we use statistics to turn patient-level cardiovascular data into evidence?
:::

## Module Overview

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-4}
### Part I  
## Descriptive Statistics

Explore the dataset, summarise patient variables, and visualise distributions.

:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### Part II  
## Hypothesis Testing

Compare patient groups and decide whether observed differences are likely to reflect meaningful effects.

:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### Part III  
## Correlation and Regression

Study relationships between clinical variables and learn how simple models support interpretation.

:::

:::

---

## Dataset at a Glance

The module uses the UCI Heart Disease dataset, a classic cardiovascular dataset containing clinical measurements, symptoms, and diagnostic information from patients investigated for heart disease.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "A simplified view of the dataset used throughout the module."

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4.5))
ax.axis("off")

items = [
    (0.15, 0.55, "Patients\n303 records"),
    (0.42, 0.75, "Demographics\nage, sex"),
    (0.42, 0.55, "Clinical measures\nBP, cholesterol"),
    (0.42, 0.35, "Symptoms & tests\nchest pain, ECG"),
    (0.75, 0.55, "Outcome\nheart disease")
]

for x, y, label in items:
    ax.text(
        x, y, label,
        ha="center", va="center", fontsize=13,
        bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.5", fc="white", ec="black", lw=1.2)
    )

ax.annotate("", xy=(0.30, 0.55), xytext=(0.22, 0.55),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", lw=1.4))

for y in [0.75, 0.55, 0.35]:
    ax.annotate("", xy=(0.66, y), xytext=(0.52, y),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", lw=1.4))

plt.show()
```
